figures.caption: Fig. 3. Base and filtered target trajectories in the transporting task.

Fig. 4. Comparison between the base policy and the filtered safe policy on the gathering task. The original and filtered control inputs, the evolution of the target distance, and the object trajectories in the XY plane are presented. The base policy induces turbulence near the end of the task, which pushes the objects farther apart and increases the distance around t ≈ 0.25s. The filtered policy mitigates this disturbance, maintains convergence toward the desired distance, and improves overall safety performance.
Fig. 1. Overall framework of the proposed boundary-safe neural operator control architecture comparing with the offline filtering baseline.
